title: Northern Shoveler
text:
View this post on Instagram















































A post shared by @poorbirdguide






When the queso cools and the pudding stiffens, you can bet your bottom dollar these waddlers will still be out plowing for glory beneath the winter sun. This is a working man’s duck (gender nonspecific), a duck that looks like a color-by-number painted by a Boy Scout. The kind of bird sat next to the antlers of a too-slow-too-fat reindeer. You need some camo pants and a sliding whistle for this birthday party. Beef jerky instead of bread crumbs if you please. This is your grandfather’s favorite duck, he just doesn’t know it. When the sky is gray and your Camry is smothered in snow, you’ll give these folks a call. They arrive in a truck that makes you question their values. Big boots, old hat, stiff smile. But don’t worry, they’re blue collar, not red collar.
And we haven’t even gotten to the star of the show yet. I mean c’mon. A certified corker and then some. The largest, flattest shnoz there ever was. They’re called spatulas for a reason. Could flip an egg with that nose. And you best believe those sneezes rock your boat. Gesundheit you marvelous scrooge. Looking like a duck from a poorly trained AI model. Add some glasses and bushy eyebrows and you’ve got yourself a third-rate comedian that can honk with the rest of ’em.
But maybe I’m just jealous. Lord knows I’d love to paddle around all day- head down, booty up as the sun shines where it was never meant to reach. And if it costs a nose that looks like the poor end of a witches curse, it might just be worth it. Paddle, eat, fly, quack, repeat. It doesn’t get much simpler than that. When you watch the ripples float off the wake of this beauty, a kind of wistfulness takes hold that’s not wholly envy or admiration but something in between. This duck is just that.

title: Canada Goose
text:
View this post on Instagram















































A post shared by @poorbirdguide






Bird nerds and jocks alike, drop your biscuits, bow your head, and plug your ears because the Canadian monarchy has arrived. Yep, you heard that right. Saddle your horses and ready your banjos, the time of incessant honking and garbling is nigh.
While it might seem that winter has already descended upon us in all directions, no season of merriment and the same 3 songs on repeat is complete without the obnoxious, intimidating, and sometimes violent Canada goose. When these deluxe squeaky chickens arrive in your backyard you’ll know it and you’ll be continually reminded of it for the next three months. All talk and full of bite. Like Will Smith at the Oscars but with slightly more dignity.
Of course, there’s something intensely fascinating about these feathered foes. Maybe it’s their streamlined V formation that would make the Blue Angels jealous or their tendency to assume we’ve created golf courses as their personal toilets. Or perhaps it’s just the immense size of them. Like a sack of potatoes on two legs some of them. But whatever it is you can’t deny that these beasts have the confidence and arrogance of a frat bro out way past his bedtime. The fearsome strut of a slicked-backed-hair, leather jacket-wearing, cliche high school bully in an early-2000s Disney show. Hold on to your lunch money kids.
And yet, nothing gets you quite ready for a nice cuppa hot cocoa in front of a cozy fire like these yapping jocks. Gaze out past the fog, admire their tenacity, and burn your tongue on a scalding mug of apple cider. This is what the holidays are all about.

title: Ian Del Tredici
text: Welcome to my homepage! I’m Ian Del Tredici, a UCSB student pursuing degrees in Environmental Studies and Ecology & Evolution. This is my professional landing page where you can find everything from my academic coursework to my personal interests. Feel free to explore!

title: Great Blue Heron
text:
View this post on Instagram















































A post shared by @poorbirdguide






I cannot believe I got a picture of this bird. This is the equivalent of finding LeBron James in your backyard. Or Harry Styles. Just pure royalty. The great blue heron is the king of all birds, and it knows it. The eye liner, the blue cap, the posture, everything just screams I’m better than you and there’s nothing you can do about it. I truly can’t believe nature kept popping out birds after this one. Like just give up at this point. You did it. You created a pristine specimen. You can’t beat it.
If you ever stop and watch a Great Blue Heron for a while (highly recommend) like your sister watches The Bachelor (no makeup, pizza rolls, full commitment) you’ll notice just how much patience and poise this bird has when hunting. I kid you not this bird could be a 3rd grade substitute teacher and those chums would be sitting on their tiny desks doing multiplication tables by 10am. Just a bird that exudes serenity. This bird that will watch a full soccer game, no breaks, no snacks, not even a twitch towards its phone at the 60th minute. Professors wish this bird would come to their classes.
All to say that anytime you see this bird just remember how high the totem pool of perfection reaches above you. You are a leftover pencil shaving compared to this heron. And not even a colored pencil either, just a plain old #3 pencil, if those even exist. Out there on the pond, where the soft sea meets the low grass, there’s nothing more regarded than a Great Blue Heron.
